subject: Mathematics
title: CBSE Class 12 Mathematics ‚ÄĒ Sample Question Paper 2025-26
_provenance: VERBATIM from the official CBSE Class XII Mathematics (Code 041) Sample Question Paper + Marking Scheme, Session 2025-26 (D-120 ‚ÄĒ retrieve, don't generate). Read eyes-on from the PDFs (pages rendered for figures and math notation). LaTeX/math notation is preserved in the prompts. For Visually-Impaired (VI) variants printed in the SQP, the VI variant is folded into the same question's prompt in square brackets and its MS answer is noted in msAnswer. Answer keys are from the Marking Scheme and are SERVER-SIDE ONLY.
labNote: No physical-lab questions in the theory paper. VSA / SA / LA / case-study items are grader 'llm-rubric' (presented + node-mapped; rubric-graded against the marking scheme), MCQ + assertion-reason are deterministically graded ('mcq') and drive placement. The paper carries 80 marks across 38 questions.

B.note: very short answer (VSA); internal choice in 21, 23

E.note: case-study based; 36 and 37 have subparts (1,1,2) with internal choice in the 2-mark subpart, 38 has two 2-mark subparts

questions.prompt: Identify the function shown in the graph (an inverse trigonometric function whose graph is plotted with range from ‚ąíŌÄ/2 to ŌÄ/2 and the indicated domain). (A) sin‚ĀĽ¬Ļ x  (B) sin‚ĀĽ¬Ļ(2x)  (C) sin‚ĀĽ¬Ļ(x/2)  (D) 2 sin‚ĀĽ¬Ļ x. [For Visually Impaired: The inverse trigonometric function whose domain is [‚ąí1/3, 1/3] is ‚ÄĒ (A) cos‚ĀĽ¬Ļ x (B) cos‚ĀĽ¬Ļ(x/3) (C) cos‚ĀĽ¬Ļ(3x) (D) 3 cos‚ĀĽ¬Ļ x.]
questions.msAnswer: B) sin‚ĀĽ¬Ļ(2x). Clearly from the graph the domain is [‚ąí1/2, 1/2], so the function is sin‚ĀĽ¬Ļ(2x). (VI: domain [‚ąí1/3, 1/3] ‚áí cos‚ĀĽ¬Ļ(3x), answer C.)

questions.prompt: If for three matrices A = [a_ij]_{m√ó4}, B = [b_ij]_{n√ó3} and C = [c_ij]_{p√óq}, the products AB and AC are both defined and are square matrices of the same order, then the values of m, n, p and q are: (A) m = q = 3 and n = p = 4  (B) m = 2, q = 3 and n = p = 4  (C) m = q = 4 and n = p = 3  (D) m = 4, p = 2 and n = q = 3.
questions.msAnswer: A) m = q = 3 and n = p = 4. AB is defined so n = 4; AC is defined so p = 4. AB and AC are square matrices of the same order, so m √ó 3 = m √ó q ‚áí q = 3 = m.

questions.prompt: If the matrix A = [[0, r, ‚ąí2], [3, p, t], [q, ‚ąí4, 0]] is skew-symmetric, then the value of (q+t)/(p+r) is ‚Ä¶. (A) ‚ąí2  (B) 0  (C) 1  (D) 2.
questions.msAnswer: A) ‚ąí2. As A is skew-symmetric, p = 0, q = 2, r = ‚ąí3, t = 4; so (q+t)/(p+r) = 6/(‚ąí3) = ‚ąí2.

questions.prompt: If A is a square matrix of order 4 and |adj A| = 27, then A (adj A) is equal to: (A) 3  (B) 9  (C) 3 I  (D) 9 I.

questions.msAnswer: B) The inverse of a diagonal matrix is the diagonal matrix of reciprocals: diag(1/3, 1/2, 1/5).

questions.prompt: Value of the determinant |[cos 67¬į, sin 67¬į], [sin 23¬į, cos 23¬į]| is (A) 0  (B) 1/2  (C) ‚ąö3/2  (D) 1.
questions.msAnswer: A) 0. The determinant = cos 67¬į cos 23¬į ‚ąí sin 67¬į sin 23¬į = cos(67¬į + 23¬į) = cos 90¬į = 0.

questions.prompt: If a function defined by f(x) = { kx + 1, x ‚Č§ ŌÄ ; cos x, x > ŌÄ } is continuous at x = ŌÄ, then the value of k is (A) ŌÄ  (B) ‚ąí1/ŌÄ  (C) 0  (D) ‚ąí2/ŌÄ.
questions.msAnswer: D) ‚ąí2/ŌÄ. Continuity at x = ŌÄ: lim_{x‚ÜíŌÄ‚ĀĽ}(kx+1) = lim_{x‚ÜíŌÄ‚Āļ} cos x = f(ŌÄ) ‚áí kŌÄ + 1 = cos ŌÄ = ‚ąí1 ‚áí k = ‚ąí2/ŌÄ.

questions.prompt: If f(x) = x tan‚ĀĽ¬Ļ x, then f‚Ä≤(1) is equal to (A) ŌÄ/4 ‚ąí 1/2  (B) ŌÄ/4 + 1/2  (C) ‚ąíŌÄ/4 ‚ąí 1/2  (D) ‚ąíŌÄ/4 + 1/2.
A function f(x) = 10 ‚ąí x ‚ąí 2x¬≤ is increasing on the interval (A) (‚ąí‚ąě, ‚ąí1/4)  (B) (‚ąí‚ąě, 1/4)  (C) [‚ąí1/4, ‚ąě)  (D) [‚ąí1/4, 1/4].
questions.msAnswer: A) (‚ąí‚ąě, ‚ąí1/4]. f‚Ä≤(x) = ‚ąí1 ‚ąí 4x; for increasing f‚Ä≤(x) ‚Č• 0 ‚áí ‚ąí(1 + 4x) ‚Č• 0 ‚áí 1 + 4x ‚Č§ 0 ‚áí x ‚Č§ ‚ąí1/4 ‚áí x ‚ąą (‚ąí‚ąě, ‚ąí1/4].

questions.prompt: The solution of the differential equation x dx + y dy = 0 represents a family of (A) straight lines  (B) parabolas  (C) circles  (D) ellipses.
questions.msAnswer: C) Circles. ‚ąęx dx = ‚ąí‚ąęy dy ‚áí x¬≤/2 = ‚ąíy¬≤/2 + k ‚áí x¬≤ + y¬≤ = 2k, k arbitrary ‚ÄĒ a family of circles.

questions.prompt: If f(a + b ‚ąí x) = f(x), then ‚ąę_a^b x f(x) dx is equal to (A) (a+b)/2 ‚ąę_a^b f(b‚ąíx) dx  (B) (a+b)/2 ‚ąę_a^b f(a‚ąíx) dx  (C) (b‚ąía)/2 ‚ąę_a^b f(x) dx  (D) (a+b)/2 ‚ąę_a^b f(x) dx.
questions.msAnswer: D) (a+b)/2 ‚ąę_a^b f(x) dx. Using I = ‚ąę_a^b x f(x) dx = ‚ąę_a^b (a+b‚ąíx) f(a+b‚ąíx) dx = ‚ąę_a^b (a+b‚ąíx) f(x) dx ‚áí 2I = (a+b)‚ąę_a^b f(x) dx ‚áí I = (a+b)/2 ‚ąę_a^b f(x) dx.

questions.prompt: If ‚ąę x¬≥ sin‚Āī(x‚Āī) cos(x‚Āī) dx = a sin‚ĀĶ(x‚Āī) + C, then a is equal to (A) ‚ąí1/10  (B) 1/20  (C) 1/4  (D) 1/5.
questions.msAnswer: B) 1/20. Let sin(x‚Āī) = t ‚áí 4x¬≥ cos(x‚Āī) dx = dt ‚áí x¬≥ cos(x‚Āī) dx = (1/4) dt; I = ‚ąę t‚Āī (1/4) dt = (1/20) t‚ĀĶ + C = (1/20) sin‚ĀĶ(x‚Āī) + C, so a = 1/20.

questions.prompt: A bird flies through a distance in a straight line given by the vector √ģ + 2ńĶ + kŐā. A man standing beside a straight metro rail track given by r‚Éó = (3 + őĽ)√ģ + (2őĽ ‚ąí 1)ńĶ + 3őĽkŐā is observing the bird. The projected length of its flight on the metro track is (A) 6/‚ąö14 units  (B) 14/‚ąö6 units  (C) 8/‚ąö14 units  (D) 5/‚ąö6 units.
questions.msAnswer: C) 8/‚ąö14 units. The projection of the vector √ģ + 2ńĶ + kŐā on the line direction √ģ + 2ńĶ + 3kŐā is (1√ó1 + 2√ó2 + 1√ó3)/‚ąö(1¬≤ + 2¬≤ + 3¬≤) = 8/‚ąö14 units.

questions.prompt: The distance of the point with position vector 3√ģ + 4ńĶ + 5kŐā from the y-axis is (A) 4 units  (B) ‚ąö34 units  (C) 5 units  (D) 5‚ąö2 units.
questions.msAnswer: B) ‚ąö34 units. The distance of the point (a, b, c) from the y-axis is ‚ąö(a¬≤ + c¬≤) = ‚ąö(3¬≤ + 5¬≤) = ‚ąö34 units.

questions.prompt: If a‚Éó = 3√ģ + 2ńĶ + 4kŐā, b‚Éó = √ģ + ńĶ ‚ąí 3kŐā and c‚Éó = 6√ģ ‚ąí ńĶ + 2kŐā are three given vectors, then (2a‚Éó¬∑√ģ)√ģ ‚ąí (b‚Éó¬∑ńĶ)ńĶ + (c‚Éó¬∑kŐā)kŐā is the same as the vector (A) a‚Éó  (B) b‚Éó + c‚Éó  (C) a‚Éó ‚ąí b‚Éó  (D) c‚Éó.
questions.msAnswer: D) c‚Éó. (2a‚Éó¬∑√ģ)√ģ ‚ąí (b‚Éó¬∑ńĶ)ńĶ + (c‚Éó¬∑kŐā)kŐā = (2√ó3)√ģ ‚ąí (1)ńĶ + (2)kŐā = 6√ģ ‚ąí ńĶ + 2kŐā = c‚Éó.

questions.prompt: A student of class XII studying Mathematics comes across an incomplete question in a book. Maximise Z = 3x + 2y + 1 subject to the constraints x ‚Č• 0, y ‚Č• 0, 3x + 4y ‚Č§ 12. He/She notices the shown graph for the said LPP problem and finds that a constraint is missing in it. Help him/her choose the required constraint from the graph. The missing constraint is (A) x + 2y ‚Č§ 2  (B) 2x + y ‚Č• 2  (C) 2x + y ‚Č§ 2  (D) x + 2y ‚Č• 2. [For Visually Impaired: If Z = ax + by + c, where a, b, c > 0, attains its maximum value at two of its corner points (4,0) and (0,3) of the feasible region determined by the system of linear inequalities, then (A) 4a = 3b (B) 3a = 4b (C) 4a + c = 3b (D) 3a + c = 4b.]
questions.msAnswer: B) 2x + y ‚Č• 2. The points (1,0) and (0,2) satisfy 2x + y = 2; the shaded region shows (0,0) does not lie in the feasible region, so the inequality is 2x + y ‚Č• 2. (VI: (4,0) and (0,3) give max ‚áí Z_{(4,0)} = Z_{(0,3)} ‚áí 4a + c = 3b + c ‚áí 4a = 3b, answer A.)

questions.prompt: The feasible region of a linear programming problem is bounded but the objective function attains its minimum value at more than one point. One of the points is (5,0). Then one of the other possible points at which the objective function attains its minimum value is (A) (2,9)  (B) (6,6)  (C) (4,7)  (D) (0,0). [For Visually Impaired: The graph of the inequality 3x + 5y < 10 is the (A) Entire XY-plane (B) Open half plane that doesn't contain origin (C) Open half plane that contains origin, but not the points of the line 3x + 5y = 10 (D) Half plane that contains origin and the points of the line 3x + 5y = 10.]
questions.msAnswer: A) (2,9). The point (2,9) can be read from the line on the graph; the line 3x + y = 15 joining (5,0) and (0,15) passes through (2,9). (VI: answer C ‚ÄĒ open half plane containing origin but not the points of the line 3x + 5y = 10.)

questions.prompt: A person observed the first 4 digits of your 6-digit PIN. What is the probability that the person can guess your PIN? (A) 1/81  (B) 1/100  (C) 1/90  (D) 1.
questions.msAnswer: B) 1/100. The person knows the first 4 digits and must guess the remaining two: P = 1√ó1√ó1√ó1√ó(1/10)√ó(1/10) = 1/100.

questions.prompt: Assertion (A): Value of the expression sin‚ĀĽ¬Ļ(‚ąö3/2) + tan‚ĀĽ¬Ļ 1 ‚ąí sec‚ĀĽ¬Ļ(‚ąö2) is ŌÄ/4. Reason (R): Principal value branch of sin‚ĀĽ¬Ļ x is [‚ąíŌÄ/2, ŌÄ/2] and that of sec‚ĀĽ¬Ļ x is [0, ŌÄ] ‚ąí {ŌÄ/2}. Choose: (A) Both A and R are true and R is the correct explanation of A; (B) Both A and R are true but R is NOT the correct explanation of A; (C) A is true but R is false; (D) A is false but R is true.
options: Both Assertion and Reason are true and Reason is the correct explanation of Assertion.
Both Assertion and Reason are true but Reason is not the correct explanation of Assertion.
Assertion is true but Reason is false.
Assertion is false but Reason is true.
questions.correct: Assertion is false but Reason is true.
questions.msAnswer: D) Assertion is false, but Reason is true. sin‚ĀĽ¬Ļ(‚ąö3/2) + tan‚ĀĽ¬Ļ 1 ‚ąí sec‚ĀĽ¬Ļ(‚ąö2) = ŌÄ/3 + ŌÄ/4 ‚ąí ŌÄ/4 = ŌÄ/3 ‚Č† ŌÄ/4, so A is false; the stated principal value branches are correct, so R is true.

questions.prompt: Assertion (A): Given two non-zero vectors a‚Éó and b‚Éó. If r‚Éó is another non-zero vector such that r‚Éó √ó (a‚Éó + b‚Éó) = 0‚Éó, then r‚Éó is perpendicular to a‚Éó √ó b‚Éó. Reason (R): The vector (a‚Éó + b‚Éó) is perpendicular to the plane of a‚Éó and b‚Éó. Choose: (A) Both A and R are true and R is the correct explanation of A; (B) Both A and R are true but R is NOT the correct explanation of A; (C) A is true but R is false; (D) A is false but R is true.
options: Both Assertion and Reason are true and Reason is the correct explanation of Assertion.
Both Assertion and Reason are true but Reason is not the correct explanation of Assertion.
Assertion is true but Reason is false.
Assertion is false but Reason is true.
questions.correct: Assertion is true but Reason is false.
questions.msAnswer: C) Assertion is true, but Reason is false. r‚Éó √ó (a‚Éó + b‚Éó) = 0‚Éó ‚áí r‚Éó is parallel to (a‚Éó + b‚Éó), which lies in the plane of a‚Éó and b‚Éó, so r‚Éó is perpendicular to a‚Éó √ó b‚Éó (A true). But (a‚Éó + b‚Éó) lies in the plane of a‚Éó and b‚Éó, so it is NOT perpendicular to that plane (R false).

questions.prompt: Evaluate tan(tan‚ĀĽ¬Ļ(‚ąí1) + ŌÄ/3). [OR ‚ÄĒ see 21B]
questions.msAnswer: tan(tan‚ĀĽ¬Ļ(‚ąí1) + ŌÄ/3) = tan(‚ąíŌÄ/4 + ŌÄ/3) = (tan(ŌÄ/3) ‚ąí tan(ŌÄ/4))/(1 + tan(ŌÄ/3)tan(ŌÄ/4)) = (‚ąö3 ‚ąí 1)/(1 + ‚ąö3) = 2 ‚ąí ‚ąö3.

questions.prompt: Find the domain of cos‚ĀĽ¬Ļ(3x ‚ąí 2). [OR of 21A ‚ÄĒ internal choice]
questions.msAnswer: For the domain, ‚ąí1 ‚Č§ 3x ‚ąí 2 ‚Č§ 1 ‚áí 1 ‚Č§ 3x ‚Č§ 3 ‚áí 1/3 ‚Č§ x ‚Č§ 1. So the domain of cos‚ĀĽ¬Ļ(3x ‚ąí 2) is [1/3, 1].

questions.prompt: If y = log tan(ŌÄ/4 + x/2), then prove that dy/dx ‚ąí sec x = 0.
questions.msAnswer: dy/dx = (1/tan(ŌÄ/4 + x/2))¬∑sec¬≤(ŌÄ/4 + x/2)¬∑(1/2) = [cos(ŌÄ/4+x/2)/sin(ŌÄ/4+x/2)]¬∑[1/cos¬≤(ŌÄ/4+x/2)]¬∑(1/2) = 1/[2 sin(ŌÄ/4+x/2)cos(ŌÄ/4+x/2)] = 1/sin(ŌÄ/2 + x) = 1/cos x = sec x. Hence dy/dx ‚ąí sec x = 0.

questions.prompt: Find: ‚ąę (x‚ąí3)/(x‚ąí1)¬≥ eň£ dx. [OR ‚ÄĒ see 23B]
questions.msAnswer: ‚ąę (x‚ąí3)eň£/(x‚ąí1)¬≥ dx = ‚ąę (x‚ąí1‚ąí2)eň£/(x‚ąí1)¬≥ dx = ‚ąę [1/(x‚ąí1)¬≤ ‚ąí 2/(x‚ąí1)¬≥] eň£ dx = ‚ąę [1/(x‚ąí1)¬≤ + d/dx(1/(x‚ąí1)¬≤)] eň£ dx = eň£/(x‚ąí1)¬≤ + c (using ‚ąę(f(x)+f‚Ä≤(x))eň£ dx = eň£ f(x) + c).

questions.prompt: Find out the area of the shaded region in the enclosed figure (region bounded by the curve x¬≤ = y, the line y = 4, and the y-axis, between A and B as shown). [OR of 23A ‚ÄĒ internal choice.] [For Visually Impaired: Find out the area of the region enclosed by the curve y¬≤ = x, x = 3 and the x-axis in the first quadrant.]
If f(x + y) = f(x)f(y) for all x, y ‚ąą R and f(5) = 2, f‚Ä≤(0) = 3, then using the definition of derivatives, find f‚Ä≤(5).

The two vectors √ģ + ńĶ + kŐā and 3√ģ ‚ąí ńĶ + 3kŐā represent the two sides OA and OB respectively of a őĒOAB, where O is the origin. The point P lies on AB such that OP is a median. Find the area of the parallelogram formed by the two adjacent sides as OA and OP.
questions.msAnswer: The vector OP = (1/2)(4√ģ + 4kŐā) = 2√ģ + 2kŐā. Area of the parallelogram with adjacent sides OA and OP = |OA √ó OP| = |[√ģ, ńĶ, kŐā; 1, 1, 1; 2, 0, 2]| = |2√ģ ‚ąí 2kŐā| = 2‚ąö2 square units.

questions.prompt: If x^y = e^{x‚ąíy}, prove that dy/dx = log x / (log(xe))¬≤ and hence find its value at x = e. [OR ‚ÄĒ see 26B]
questions.msAnswer: x^y = e^{x‚ąíy}; take log: y log x = (x ‚ąí y) log e ‚áí y log x + y = x (since log e = 1) ‚áí y = x/(1 + log x). Differentiating: dy/dx = [(1 + log x)¬∑1 ‚ąí x¬∑(1/x)]/(1 + log x)¬≤ = log x/(1 + log x)¬≤ = log x/(log e + log x)¬≤ = log x/(log(xe))¬≤. At x = e: dy/dx = log e/(log e¬≤)¬≤ = 1/(2 log e)¬≤ = 1/2¬≤ = 1/4.

questions.prompt: If x = a(őł ‚ąí sin őł), y = a(1 ‚ąí cos őł), find d¬≤y/dx¬≤. [OR of 26A ‚ÄĒ internal choice.]
questions.msAnswer: dx/dőł = a(1 ‚ąí cos őł), dy/dőł = a(0 + sin őł); dy/dx = a sin őł/[a(1 ‚ąí cos őł)] = 2 sin(őł/2)cos(őł/2)/[2 sin¬≤(őł/2)] = cot(őł/2). d¬≤y/dx¬≤ = ‚ąí(1/2) cosec¬≤(őł/2)¬∑(dőł/dx) = ‚ąí(1/2) cosec¬≤(őł/2)¬∑1/[2 a sin¬≤(őł/2)] = ‚ąí(1/4a) cosec‚Āī(őł/2).

questions.prompt: A spherical ball of ice melts in such a way that the rate at which its volume decreases at any instant is directly proportional to its surface area. Prove that the radius of the ice ball decreases at a constant rate.
questions.msAnswer: Let r be the radius at time t. V = (4/3)ŌÄr¬≥ ‚Ä¶ (1), S = 4ŌÄr¬≤ ‚Ä¶ (2). Given dV/dt ‚ąĚ S ‚áí dV/dt = ‚ąíkS (k > 0) ‚Ä¶ (3). Differentiating (1): dV/dt = (4/3)ŌÄ¬∑3r¬≤¬∑(dr/dt) = 4ŌÄr¬≤(dr/dt) ‚Ä¶ (4). From (3) and (4): ‚ąíkS = 4ŌÄr¬≤(dr/dt); using (2), ‚ąíkS = S(dr/dt) ‚áí dr/dt = ‚ąík, a constant. Hence the radius decreases at a constant rate.

questions.prompt: Sketch the graph y = |x + 1|. Evaluate ‚ąę_{‚ąí4}^{2} |x + 1| dx. What does the value of this integral represent on the graph? [OR ‚ÄĒ see 28B.] [For Visually Impaired: Define the function y = |x + 1|. Evaluate ‚ąę_{‚ąí4}^{2} |x + 1| dx. What does the value of this integral represent?]
questions.msAnswer: ‚ąę_{‚ąí4}^{2} |x + 1| dx = ‚ąę_{‚ąí4}^{‚ąí1} ‚ąí(x + 1) dx + ‚ąę_{‚ąí1}^{2} (x + 1) dx = ‚ąí[(x+1)¬≤/2]_{‚ąí4}^{‚ąí1} + [(x+1)¬≤/2]_{‚ąí1}^{2} = ‚ąí(0 ‚ąí 9/2) + (9/2 ‚ąí 0) = 9. It represents the area of the region bounded by the curve y = |x + 1|, the x-axis and the lines x = ‚ąí4 and x = 2.

questions.prompt: Using integration find the area of the region {(x, y) : x¬≤ ‚ąí 4y ‚Č§ 0, y ‚ąí x ‚Č§ 0}. [OR of 28A ‚ÄĒ internal choice.] [For Visually Impaired: Using integration find the area enclosed within the curve 25x¬≤ + 16y¬≤ = 400.]
Find the distance of the point (2, ‚ąí1, 3) from the line r‚Éó = (2√ģ ‚ąí ńĶ + 2kŐā) + őľ(3√ģ + 6ńĶ + 2kŐā) measured parallel to the z-axis. [OR ‚ÄĒ see 29B]
questions.msAnswer: The line through (2, ‚ąí1, 3) parallel to the z-axis is r‚Éó = (2√ģ ‚ąí ńĶ + 3kŐā) + őĽ(kŐā); any point on it is P(2, ‚ąí1, 3 + őĽ). Any point on the given line is Q(2 + 3őľ, ‚ąí1 + 6őľ, 2 + 2őľ). For the intersection Q = P: 2 = 2 + 3őľ ‚áí őľ = 0, so the point of intersection is (2, ‚ąí1, 2). The distance from (2, ‚ąí1, 3) to (2, ‚ąí1, 2) is 1 unit.

questions.prompt: Find the point of intersection of the line r‚Éó = (3√ģ + kŐā) + őľ(√ģ + ńĶ + kŐā) and the line through (2, ‚ąí1, 1) parallel to the z-axis. How far is this point from the z-axis? [OR of 29A ‚ÄĒ internal choice.]
questions.msAnswer: The line through (2, ‚ąí1, 1) parallel to the z-axis is r‚Éó = (2√ģ ‚ąí ńĶ + kŐā) + őĽ(kŐā); any point on it is P(2, ‚ąí1, 1 + őĽ). Any point on the given line is A(3 + őľ, őľ, 1 + őľ). Setting A = P: őľ = ‚ąí1, so the point of intersection is (2, ‚ąí1, 0). The distance of (2, ‚ąí1, 0) from the z-axis is ‚ąö(2¬≤ + (‚ąí1)¬≤) = ‚ąö5 units.

questions.prompt: Solve graphically: Maximise Z = 2x + y subject to x + y ‚Č§ 1200, x + y ‚Č• 600, y ‚Č§ x/2, x ‚Č• 0, y ‚Č• 0. [For Visually Impaired: The objective function Z = 3x + 2y of a linear programming problem under some constraints is to be maximized and minimized. The corner points of the feasible region are A(600,0), B(1200,0), C(800,400) and D(400,200). Find the point at which Z is maximum and the point at which Z is minimum. Also find the corresponding maximum and minimum values of Z.]
questions.msAnswer: Sketching the graph, the corner points are A(600,0), B(1200,0), C(800,400), D(400,200). Values of Z = 2x + y: Z_A = 1200, Z_B = 2400, Z_C = 2000, Z_D = 1000. Maximum Z = 2400 when x = 1200 and y = 0. (VI: with Z = 3x + 2y, Z_A = 1800, Z_B = 3600, Z_C = 3200, Z_D = 1600; maximum = 3600 at B(1200,0) and minimum = 1600 at D(400,200).)

questions.prompt: Two students Mehul and Rashi are seeking admission in a college. The probability that Mehul is selected is 0.4 and the probability of selection of exactly one of them is 0.5. Chances of selection of them are independent of each other. Find the chances of selection of Rashi. Also find the probability of selection of at least one of them.
questions.msAnswer: Let A: Mehul selected, B: Rashi selected; A and B independent, P(A) = 0.4, P(A ‚ą© BŐĄ) + P(B ‚ą© ńÄ) = 0.5. Let P(B) = x. Then P(A)P(BŐĄ) + P(B)P(ńÄ) = 0.5 ‚áí 0.4(1 ‚ąí x) + x(1 ‚ąí 0.4) = 0.5 ‚áí 0.4 ‚ąí 0.4x + 0.6x = 0.5 ‚áí 0.2x = 0.1 ‚áí x = 0.5. So P(Rashi) = 0.5. P(at least one) = 1 ‚ąí P(ńÄ ‚ą© BŐĄ) = 1 ‚ąí P(ńÄ)P(BŐĄ) = 1 ‚ąí 0.6 √ó 0.5 = 1 ‚ąí 0.3 = 0.7.

questions.prompt: For two matrices A = [[3, ‚ąí6, ‚ąí1], [2, ‚ąí5, ‚ąí1], [‚ąí2, 4, 1]] and B = [[1, ‚ąí2, ‚ąí1], [0, ‚ąí1, ‚ąí1], [2, 0, 3]], find the product AB and hence solve the system of equations: 3x ‚ąí 6y ‚ąí z = 3, 2x ‚ąí 5y ‚ąí z + 2 = 0, ‚ąí2x + 4y + z = 5.
questions.msAnswer: AB = [[3,‚ąí6,‚ąí1],[2,‚ąí5,‚ąí1],[‚ąí2,4,1]]¬∑[[1,‚ąí2,‚ąí1],[0,‚ąí1,‚ąí1],[2,0,3]] = [[1,0,0],[0,1,0],[0,0,1]] = I. So A‚ĀĽ¬Ļ = B and B‚ĀĽ¬Ļ = A. The system 3x ‚ąí 6y ‚ąí z = 3, 2x ‚ąí 5y ‚ąí z = ‚ąí2, ‚ąí2x + 4y + z = 5 in matrix form AX = C with X = [x; y; z], C = [3; ‚ąí2; 5]. Here |A| = ‚ąí1 ‚Č† 0, so the system is consistent with unique solution X = A‚ĀĽ¬ĻC = BC = [[1,‚ąí2,‚ąí1],[0,‚ąí1,‚ąí1],[2,0,3]]¬∑[3;‚ąí2;5] = [2; ‚ąí3; 21]. Thus x = 2, y = ‚ąí3, z = 21.

questions.prompt: Evaluate: ‚ąę_0^1 log(1 + x)/(1 + x¬≤) dx. [OR ‚ÄĒ see 33B]
questions.msAnswer: Let x = tan őł ‚áí dx = sec¬≤őł dőł; I = ‚ąę_0^{ŌÄ/4} log(1 + tan őł)/(1 + tan¬≤őł)¬∑sec¬≤őł dőł = ‚ąę_0^{ŌÄ/4} log(1 + tan őł) dőł = ‚ąę_0^{ŌÄ/4} log[1 + tan(ŌÄ/4 ‚ąí őł)] dőł = ‚ąę_0^{ŌÄ/4} log[1 + (1 ‚ąí tan őł)/(1 + tan őł)] dőł = ‚ąę_0^{ŌÄ/4} log[2/(1 + tan őł)] dőł = ‚ąę_0^{ŌÄ/4} log 2 dőł ‚ąí ‚ąę_0^{ŌÄ/4} log(1 + tan őł) dőł = (log 2)(ŌÄ/4) ‚ąí I ‚áí 2I = (ŌÄ/4) log 2 ‚áí I = (ŌÄ/8) log 2.

questions.prompt: Find ‚ąę (3 sin őł ‚ąí 2) cos őł / (5 ‚ąí cos¬≤őł ‚ąí 4 sin őł) dőł. [OR of 33A ‚ÄĒ internal choice.]
questions.msAnswer: I = ‚ąę (3 sin őł ‚ąí 2) cos őł/(5 ‚ąí cos¬≤őł ‚ąí 4 sin őł) dőł = ‚ąę (3 sin őł ‚ąí 2) cos őł/(5 ‚ąí (1 ‚ąí sin¬≤őł) ‚ąí 4 sin őł) dőł. Let sin őł = t ‚áí cos őł dőł = dt; I = ‚ąę (3t ‚ąí 2)/(t¬≤ ‚ąí 4t + 4) dt = ‚ąę (3t ‚ąí 2)/(t ‚ąí 2)¬≤ dt. Partial fractions: (3t ‚ąí 2)/(t ‚ąí 2)¬≤ = A/(t ‚ąí 2) + B/(t ‚ąí 2)¬≤ ‚áí 3t ‚ąí 2 = A(t ‚ąí 2) + B; comparing, A = 3, ‚ąí2A + B = ‚ąí2 ‚áí B = 4. So I = ‚ąę 3/(t ‚ąí 2) dt + ‚ąę 4/(t ‚ąí 2)¬≤ dt = 3 log|t ‚ąí 2| ‚ąí 4/(t ‚ąí 2) + C = 3 log|sin őł ‚ąí 2| ‚ąí 4/(sin őł ‚ąí 2) + C.

questions.prompt: Solve the differential equation: y + d/dx(xy) = x(sin x + x). [OR ‚ÄĒ see 34B]
questions.msAnswer: y + d/dx(xy) = x(sin x + x) ‚áí y + (x dy/dx + y) = x(sin x + x) ‚áí 2y + x dy/dx = x(sin x + x) ‚áí dy/dx + (2/x)y = (sin x + x). This is linear with P = 2/x, Q = (sin x + x). I.F. = e^{‚ąę(2/x)dx} = e^{2 log x} = x¬≤. Solution: y¬∑I.F. = ‚ąę Q¬∑I.F. dx ‚áí yx¬≤ = ‚ąę(sin x + x)x¬≤ dx = ‚ąę x¬≤ sin x dx + ‚ąę x¬≥ dx = ‚ąíx¬≤ cos x + 2‚ąę x cos x dx + x‚Āī/4 + C = ‚ąíx¬≤ cos x + 2(x sin x + cos x) + x‚Āī/4 + C, which is the required solution.

questions.prompt: Find the particular solution of the differential equation: 2y e^{x/y} dx + (y ‚ąí 2x e^{x/y}) dy = 0, given that y(0) = 1.
questions.msAnswer: dx/dy = (2x e^{x/y} ‚ąí y)/(2y e^{x/y}) = (2(x/y)e^{x/y} ‚ąí 1)/(2 e^{x/y}) ‚ÄĒ homogeneous. Let x = vy ‚áí dx/dy = v + y dv/dy; v + y dv/dy = (2v e^v ‚ąí 1)/(2 e^v) ‚áí y dv/dy = (2v e^v ‚ąí 1)/(2 e^v) ‚ąí v = ‚ąí1/(2 e^v) ‚áí 2 e^v dv = ‚ąídy/y ‚áí ‚ąę 2 e^v dv = ‚ąí‚ąę dy/y ‚áí 2 e^v = ‚ąílog|y| + C ‚áí 2 e^{x/y} + log|y| = C. When x = 0, y = 1: 2 e^0 + 0 = C ‚áí C = 2. Required solution: 2 e^{x/y} + log|y| = 2.

questions.prompt: The two lines (x ‚ąí 1)/3 = ‚ąíy, z + 1 = 0 and ‚ąíx/2 = (y + 1)/2 = z + 2 intersect at a point whose y-coordinate is 1. Find the co-ordinates of their point of intersection. Find the vector equation of a line perpendicular to both the given lines and passing through this point of intersection.
questions.msAnswer: Let (x ‚ąí 1)/3 = (y ‚ąí 0)/(‚ąí1) = (z + 1)/0 = őĽ ‚áí any point on it is (3őĽ + 1, ‚ąíőĽ, ‚ąí1). For the point where y = 1 ‚áí őĽ = ‚ąí1 ‚áí the point is (‚ąí2, 1, ‚ąí1). The directions of the two lines are m‚Éó = 3√ģ ‚ąí ńĶ and n‚Éó = ‚ąí2√ģ + 2ńĶ + kŐā. m‚Éó √ó n‚Éó = |[√ģ, ńĶ, kŐā; 3, ‚ąí1, 0; ‚ąí2, 2, 1]| = ‚ąí√ģ ‚ąí 3ńĶ + 4kŐā. The required line is r‚Éó = (‚ąí2√ģ + ńĶ ‚ąí kŐā) + őľ(‚ąí√ģ ‚ąí 3ńĶ + 4kŐā).

questions.prompt: Case Study 1: A city's traffic management department is planning to optimize traffic flow by analyzing the connectivity between five major spots labelled A, B, C, D and E. One-way traffic flow data: (1) Traffic flows from A to B, A to C, and A to D. (2) Traffic flows from B to C and B to E. (3) Traffic flows from C to E. (4) Traffic flows from D to E and D to C. Represent and analyze this data using relations and functions.

parts.prompt: Is the traffic flow reflexive? Justify.
parts.msAnswer: Traffic flow is not reflexive, as (A, A) ‚ąČ R (no major spot is connected with itself).

parts.prompt: Is the traffic flow transitive? Justify.
parts.msAnswer: Traffic flow is not transitive, as (A, B) ‚ąą R and (B, E) ‚ąą R, but (A, E) ‚ąČ R.

parts.prompt: Represent the relation describing the traffic flow as a set of ordered pairs. Also state the domain and range of the relation. [OR ‚ÄĒ III B.]
parts.msAnswer: R = {(A,B), (A,C), (A,D), (B,C), (B,E), (C,E), (D,E), (D,C)}. Domain = {A, B, C, D}; Range = {B, C, D, E}.

parts.prompt: Does the traffic flow represent a function? Justify your answer. [OR of III A ‚ÄĒ internal choice.]
parts.msAnswer: No, the traffic flow doesn't represent a function as A has three images (B, C and D).

questions.msAnswer: I: not reflexive ((A,A) ‚ąČ R). II: not transitive ((A,B), (B,E) ‚ąą R but (A,E) ‚ąČ R). III A: R = {(A,B),(A,C),(A,D),(B,C),(B,E),(C,E),(D,E),(D,C)}, Domain {A,B,C,D}, Range {B,C,D,E}. III B: not a function (A has three images).

questions.prompt: Case Study 2: A company manufactures a new type of energy-efficient LED bulb. The cost of production and the revenue generated by selling x bulbs (in an hour) are modelled as C(x) = 0.5x¬≤ ‚ąí 10x + 150 and R(x) = ‚ąí0.3x¬≤ + 20x respectively, where C(x) and R(x) are both in ‚āĻ. To maximize the profit, analyze these functions using calculus.

parts.prompt: Find the critical points of P(x).
Determine whether the critical points correspond to a maximum or a minimum profit by using the second derivative test. [OR ‚ÄĒ III B.]
parts.msAnswer: P‚Ä≥(x) = ‚ąí1.6; in particular P‚Ä≥(18.75) = ‚ąí1.6 < 0, so the critical value x = 18.75 corresponds to a maximum profit.

parts.prompt: Identify the possible practical value of x (the number of bulbs that can realistically be produced and sold) that can maximize the profit, if the resources available and the expenditure on machines allows to produce minimum 10 but not more than 18 bulbs per hour. Also calculate the maximum profit. [OR of III A ‚ÄĒ internal choice.]
parts.msAnswer: As x is the number of bulbs, practically 18 bulbs correspond to a maximum profit (on [10, 18]). Maximum profit = P(18) = ‚ąí0.8 √ó 18¬≤ + 30 √ó 18 ‚ąí 150 = ‚ąí259.2 + 540 ‚ąí 150 = ‚āĻ130.80.

questions.msAnswer: I: P(x) = ‚ąí0.8x¬≤ + 30x ‚ąí 150. II: critical point x = 18.75. III A: P‚Ä≥(x) = ‚ąí1.6 < 0 ‚áí maximum at x = 18.75. III B: on [10,18] max at x = 18, maximum profit P(18) = ‚āĻ130.80.

questions.prompt: Case Study 3: In a class of students of the age group 14 to 17, the students were categorised into three groups by a feedback form. The first group spent more than 4 hours per day on mobile/gaming screens, the second group spent 2 to 4 hours/day, and the third group spent less than 2 hours/day. The first (high screen time) group is 60% of all students, the second (moderate) is 30%, and the third (low) is 10% of the total. It was observed that 80% of the first group faced severe anxiety and low retention issues, with 70% of the second group, and 30% of the third group having the same symptoms.

parts.prompt: What is the total percentage of students who suffer from anxiety and low retention issues in the class?
parts.msAnswer: Let E‚āĀ, E‚āā, E‚āÉ be the events of being in the first, second, third group; A the event of showing symptoms. P(E‚āĀ) = 60/100, P(E‚āā) = 30/100, P(E‚āÉ) = 10/100; P(A|E‚āĀ) = 80/100, P(A|E‚āā) = 70/100, P(A|E‚āÉ) = 30/100. P(A) = P(E‚āĀ)P(A|E‚āĀ) + P(E‚āā)P(A|E‚āā) + P(E‚āÉ)P(A|E‚āÉ) = (60/100)(80/100) + (30/100)(70/100) + (10/100)(30/100) = 72/100 = 72%.

parts.prompt: A student is selected at random, and he is found to suffer from anxiety and low retention issues. What is the probability that he/she spends screen time more than 4 hours per day?

questions.msAnswer: I: P(A) = 72%. II: P(E‚āĀ|A) = 48/72 = 2/3 (Bayes' theorem).
